title: China-to-Bangladesh BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Basis and Site-Specific Interconnection
rows.target_requirement: Bangladesh and China both operate on a 50 Hz power-system basis, which helps PCS and transformer screening, but grid interconnection still depends on the Bangladesh point of connection. Utility review can require protection settings, voltage and frequency operating windows, anti-islanding, fault ride-through, harmonics, grounding, SCADA, metering, communications, commissioning tests, and site acceptance.
target_standards: Project-specific protection, power-quality, and metering studies
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 36558-2023 system evidence and China 50 Hz design settings may support engineering review, but they are not Bangladesh utility approval. PCS firmware, transformer taps, relay settings, harmonic limits, grounding, HVAC derating, and SCADA should be validated for the Bangladesh site.
rows.gap: Produce Bangladesh-specific relay settings, grid model, power-quality study, short-circuit study, SCADA protocol, commissioning plan, and site acceptance records instead of relying only on China factory settings.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS where utility interconnection, grid-code, EPC, owner, insurer, or commissioning approval applies; details are project and connection-point specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected projects through grid-company technical review, connection agreement, and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] The 50 Hz match is helpful engineering context, not an interconnection approval. Bangladesh site settings and utility commissioning remain separate requirements.

source.publisher: Power Division, Ministry of Power, Energy and Mineral Resources, Government of Bangladesh

rows.requirement: BSTI Scope Screening and IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 Evidence
rows.target_requirement: BSTI is Bangladesh's national standards and conformity-assessment authority. Public official sources should be checked for the exact HS code, product family, and component list because a lithium BESS as a complete assembled system is not clearly established as a blanket mandatory BSTI certification item from public sources. IEC 62619:2022 battery evidence and IEC 62933 electrical energy storage system evidence may be requested by the importer, tender, owner, insurer, utility, inspection body, or a confirmed Bangladesh Standards route.
target_standards: Bangladesh Standards and Testing Institution Act, 2018
BSTI certification or import route where the exact product or component is in scope
rows.china_equivalent: China GB evidence under GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, and GB/T 36558-2023 can support the technical file, but it does not prove BSTI certification, Bangladesh Standards adoption, or acceptance for the exact shipment unless the Bangladesh route confirms it.
rows.gap: Obtain written route confirmation before shipment: whether Bangladesh requires a BSTI certificate, Bangladesh Standard evidence, direct IEC report, clause-mapped China GB report, recognized-lab report, or only project due-diligence documents.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where the exact BESS, component, HS code, import route, tender, contract, inspection body, utility, or authority decision places it in BSTI or Bangladesh Standards scope; otherwise IEC evidence is normally project or due-diligence evidence
mandatory_status.cn: China GB evidence may be mandatory or recommended depending on the exact China standard and product scope; it remains China-side evidence unless Bangladesh accepts it

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat BSTI as a scope-confirmation gate, not as an assumed blanket BESS approval. Confirm whether IEC 62619:2022, IEC 62933, or mapped China GB evidence is accepted for the exact Bangladesh route.

source.publisher: Legislative and Parliamentary Affairs Division, Government of Bangladesh

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Evidence Versus Bangladesh Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Bangladesh acceptance of China GB evidence must be confirmed through the exact BSTI, importer, tender, customs, utility, fire, insurer, owner, or contract route. Public sources do not support treating GB 44240-2024 or GB/T 36276-2023 as automatic Bangladesh whole-product BESS approval.
target_standards: Bangladesh BSTI, customs, utility, fire, transport, and project requirements applicable to the exact shipment or site
IEC 62619:2022 or IEC 62933 where the Bangladesh route requests IEC evidence
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 is China-side mandatory where its lithium-ion cells and batteries for electric energy storage scope applies. GB/T 36276-2023 is China-side recommended national standard evidence commonly used for lithium-ion batteries for electric energy storage.
rows.gap: Create a clause matrix from GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 to the Bangladesh-requested IEC, BSTI, fire, transport, grid, or project clauses, and mark which documents are technical evidence rather than local approval.

mandatory_status.target: Not confirmed as a blanket Bangladesh whole-product BESS compulsory certification; mandatory only where the Bangladesh route, authority, importer, customs process, tender, insurer, utility, or contract requires or accepts the mapped evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where its China scope applies; GB/T 36276-2023 is recommended unless incorporated by project, contract, or authority route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 as China battery-safety baseline evidence, not as automatic Bangladesh BESS approval.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform

rows.requirement: Bangladesh Fire-Safety Review for BESS Sites
rows.target_requirement: BESS installations in Bangladesh should be screened for building, industrial, warehouse, factory, power-project, and local fire-authority requirements. Review may include site layout, separation, access, ventilation or gas management, fire detection and suppression, emergency shutdown, signage, fire-service access, hazardous-material information, and emergency response procedures.
target_standards: Bangladesh Fire Prevention and Extinction Act, 2003
Fire Service and Civil Defence clearance or local fire-authority review where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, and project fire-safety review for stationary energy storage. These documents can support Bangladesh due diligence but do not replace Bangladesh local fire clearance, site approval, insurer review, or owner acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare installation drawings, fire-protection design, thermal-runaway mitigation evidence, ventilation or gas-detection rationale, emergency shutdown logic, monitoring records, SDS, and local-language emergency procedures for the Bangladesh project reviewer and fire authority.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where fire-code, factory, building, warehouse, power-project, local fire-authority, insurer, owner, or permit review applies; project-specific for small standalone equipment
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval and local fire review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Battery test reports do not close the Bangladesh site fire-safety gap. Treat fire review as a site and project approval issue.

source.publisher: Legislative and Parliamentary Affairs Division, Government of Bangladesh

rows.requirement: SREDA, Renewable Project, and Utility Storage Review
rows.target_requirement: Bangladesh storage attached to solar, net-metering, mini-grid, captive, distribution, or utility projects should be reviewed under the applicable SREDA policy or guideline, utility interconnection process, project approval, power-purchase, metering, dispatch, and commissioning documents. A product certificate alone does not decide whether the storage asset is permitted to inject, absorb, provide backup, or provide grid services.
target_standards: SREDA renewable-energy policy and guideline route applicable to the project
Bangladesh net metering guideline where solar-plus-storage or customer interconnection is in scope
Utility interconnection, metering, commissioning, and dispatch requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China grid approvals and GB/T 36558-2023 system reports can support a technical package, but they do not create Bangladesh SREDA approval, utility approval, tariff treatment, metering permission, or dispatch rights.
rows.gap: Classify the Bangladesh project role first: behind-the-meter backup, solar-plus-storage, net metering, captive generation, distribution asset, utility asset, mini-grid, or ancillary-service resource can trigger different SREDA, utility, tariff, metering, and commissioning expectations.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the BESS is part of a regulated renewable-energy, net-metering, utility, tariff, grid-connection, or project-approval route; project-specific for standalone customer-side backup equipment
mandatory_status.cn: China grid approval obligations depend on project type, voltage level, market role, and grid-connection contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Define the Bangladesh storage use case before claiming SREDA, utility, tariff, or interconnection requirements. China grid documentation is supporting evidence, not Bangladesh approval.

source.publisher: Sustainable and Renewable Energy Development Authority (SREDA)

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to Bangladesh should have UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, subsection 38.3 evidence, including a test summary made available through the transport chain. The report and summary must match the shipped design type, model, chemistry, capacity, protection design, module configuration, and change-control history.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, subsection 38.3
UN 38.3 test-summary availability through the transport chain
rows.china_equivalent: China GB battery-safety reports under GB 44240-2024 or GB/T 36276-2023 are product-safety evidence, not dangerous-goods transport evidence. Export logistics files should reconcile China design-change records with the tested UN 38.3 design and the Bangladesh-bound shipment.
rows.gap: Before booking, verify the UN 38.3 report, test summary, manufacturer and model identity, design revision, SDS, classification, packing instruction, emergency information, and carrier or port requirements for the actual route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for lithium battery dangerous-goods transport where UN model rules and modal air, sea, road, port, carrier, customs, or freight-forwarder practice apply
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory in practice for China export transport acceptance where lithium batteries are offered for transport under dangerous-goods rules

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Bangladesh project or market-access evidence and China GB battery reports do not replace UN 38.3 transport evidence for lithium batteries.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
